hero.title: Operator Notes
hero.description: Essays, case studies, and field notes from running data products and internet businesses in Russia for 30+ years.

posts.title: 20M Rubles a Year on Web Scraping
posts.excerpt: How an engineering team turned a parsing infrastructure into a sustainable B2B business collecting only public data. Real revenue breakdown, client structure, and legal framework.

posts.title: From 0 to 430,000 RUB/Month. SaaS in Russia, Slowly
posts.excerpt: A month-by-month account of growing a SaaS product in Russia without venture capital or paid advertising. Including the failures, the plateaus, and what actually moved the needle.

posts.title: How clickfraud.ru Won an 8M Ruble State Grant
posts.excerpt: We applied to the Foundation for Innovation Assistance and won. Here is how we evaluated the market, structured the application, and defended the project before technical experts.

posts.title: Inside a Click Fraud Detection System
posts.excerpt: How clickfraud.ru uses machine learning and behavioral analysis to separate real clicks from bot traffic. Architecture, signals, and the ongoing arms race against fraudsters.

posts.title: Why a Site Redesign Can Destroy Your SEO Traffic
posts.excerpt: We redesigned a site and lost 40% of organic traffic. A technical post-mortem on what went wrong and what to check before any major design overhaul.

posts.title: Zero to 40M: Building a Software and Parsing Business
posts.excerpt: The full story of how we built a portfolio of internet products — from early sysadmin days to running a company with 40M+ RUB annual revenue. Every real number included.
